engineIntelligence.h2: Dodge Grand Caravan Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Dodge Grand Caravan in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data for US-import models.

cards.altText: Dodge Grand Caravan ERB engine - petrol 3.6L replacement UK
productionHistory: Final-generation Grand Caravan models with Pentastar V6
knownFailures: Rocker arm failure - causes ticking noise and potential cylinder head damage from 60k miles.
Cylinder head cracks - documented on 2011-2014 ERB units leading to misfires and oil consumption.

cards.altText: Dodge Grand Caravan EGH engine - petrol 3.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - common on higher-mileage units causing overheating and white exhaust smoke.
Stalling at idle - documented issue linked to sensor or PCM faults on 2007-2010 examples.

cards.altText: Dodge Grand Caravan EGV engine - petrol 3.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - causes rattle on start-up and potential chain skip if neglected.
Oil pressure loss - documented on high-mileage units leading to bearing wear.

engineGuide.h2: Dodge Grand Caravan Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Dodge Grand Caravan across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: The Pentastar ERB replaced older 3.8L units, bringing 283hp and FlexFuel capability - but also rocker arm issues that dominate UK replacement demand.
commonFailures: Rocker arm failure - causes ticking noise and potential cylinder head damage from 60k miles.
Cylinder head cracks - documented on 2011-2014 ERB units leading to misfires.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERB Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement

entries.title: 3.8L Petrol
entries.history: The EGH 3.8L was the workhorse of early Grand Caravans, offering reliable torque but prone to head gasket issues on higher-mileage imports.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - common on higher-mileage units causing overheating and white exhaust smoke.
Stalling at idle - documented issue linked to sensor or PCM faults on 2007-2010 examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EGH Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement

entries.title: 3.8L Petrol
entries.history: The EGL variant of the 3.8L offered minor calibration updates over EGH, commonly found in 2007-2010 SXT models with similar failure patterns.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - shared with EGH, causes coolant loss and overheating.
P0339/P0344 sensor codes - wiring or PCM issues triggering stalling.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EGL Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement

entries.title: 3.3L Petrol
entries.history: The entry-level 3.3L EGV powered base Grand Caravans, offering economy but requiring careful maintenance to avoid timing chain issues.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - causes rattle on start-up and potential chain skip if neglected.
Oil pressure loss - documented on high-mileage units leading to bearing wear.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EGV Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement

entries.title: 3.3L Petrol
entries.history: The earlier EGA 3.3L powered 2000-2006 Grand Caravans, a simple pushrod design with fewer electronics but age-related wear concerns.
commonFailures: Oil pump wear - leads to low oil pressure warnings on high-mileage examples.
Valve stem seal degradation - causes oil consumption and blue exhaust smoke.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EGA Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: The high-output EGQ 4.0L was offered in RT trims 2007-2010, delivering 257hp but with limited UK availability and specialist rebuild requirements.
commonFailures: Intake manifold gasket failure - causes rough idle and vacuum leaks on older units.
Coolant crossover leaks - documented on high-mileage EGQ engines.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EGQ Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement

Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Dodge Grand Caravan Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Dodge Grand Caravan's petrol V6 engines are generally robust, but the 3.6L Pentastar (ERB) and 3.8L (EGH/EGL) units dominate UK replacement demand due to rocker arm failures and head gasket issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide between repair and replacement.

problems.group: Dodge Grand Caravan 3.6L Pentastar Ticking Noise
problems.h4: Dodge Grand Caravan 3.6L Pentastar Ticking Noise - Why ERB Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Grand Caravan SXT, RT, Crew, SE (2011-2016, ERB Pentastar 3.6L)
problems.rootCause: Rocker arm wear causes valve train ticking; unchecked, this leads to cylinder head damage and misfires requiring full engine replacement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace rocker arms, inspect cylinder heads, update valve train components
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before head damage; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove heads, machine surfaces, replace valves/gaskets, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life if block is sound; not suitable if block is scored or cracked

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt ERB engine with upgraded rocker arms, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage vans; resolves all internal wear in one solution

problems.recommendation: On a Grand Caravan worth £3,000-£8,000, a preventative rocker arm service is only worthwhile below 70k miles with no misfire codes. Once cylinder head damage is confirmed, a rebuilt ERB engine from our network offers better long-term value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt ERB Pentastar engine prices for your Dodge Grand Caravan

problems.group: Dodge Grand Caravan 3.8L Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: Dodge Grand Caravan 3.8L Head Gasket Failure - Why EGH/EGL Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Grand Caravan SE, SXT, Sport (2004-2010, EGH/EGL 3.8L)
problems.rootCause: Age-related gasket degradation combined with cooling system neglect leads to coolant leakage into cylinders, causing overheating and potential block damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace head gaskets, resurface if needed, refill coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block and heads are undamaged; risk of repeat failure on aged components

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EGH/EGL engine with updated gaskets, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for vans over 100k miles; resolves all age-related wear in one installation

problems.recommendation: Head gasket repair on a 3.8L Grand Caravan is only cost-effective if the vehicle is under 90k miles and has full service history. For higher-mileage imports, a rebuilt engine from our specialist network provides greater peace of mind and warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 3.8L EGH/EGL engines for your Grand Caravan

problems.group: Dodge Grand Caravan Stalling at Idle
problems.h4: Dodge Grand Caravan Stalling at Idle - Sensor & PCM Faults
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Grand Caravan SE, SXT (2007-2010, EGH/EGL 3.8L)
problems.rootCause: Crankshaft/camshaft position sensor circuits (P0339/P0344 codes) or PCM wiring faults cause intermittent stalling; repeated stalling can damage catalytic converters and increase replacement complexity.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace crank/cam sensors, inspect wiring looms, clear fault codes, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if stalling is intermittent and no internal damage; diagnostic essential first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EGH/EGL engine if stalling caused internal damage; includes new sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if stalling led to hydrolock, bearing wear, or catalytic damage

problems.recommendation: Always start with a full diagnostic to isolate sensor vs internal issues. If codes clear and stalling stops after sensor replacement, further work may not be needed. If misfires persist, a rebuilt engine may be the more reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement options for stalling issues

problems.group: Dodge Grand Caravan Loss of Power on Acceleration
problems.h4: Dodge Grand Caravan Loss of Power on Acceleration - Internal Wear
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Grand Caravan SXT, RT, Crew (2011-2016, ERB 3.6L; 2004-2010, EGH/EGL 3.8L)
problems.rootCause: Worn piston rings, valve guides, or timing components reduce compression and power output; often accompanied by increased oil consumption or unusual noises.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valves, guides, seals, timing components; machine heads if needed
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if block compression is good; addresses power loss from valve train wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt engine with upgraded internals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for comprehensive power restoration on high-mileage Grand Caravans

problems.recommendation: Power loss accompanied by oil consumption or knocking noises typically indicates bottom-end wear. In these cases, a rebuilt engine offers better value than partial repairs, especially for US-import vans where parts availability can delay repairs.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt Dodge Grand Caravan engines that restore full power

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Dodge Grand Caravan
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with Pentastar ticking, 3.8L head gasket issues, or sensor-related stalling, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Dodge Grand Caravan specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Dodge Grand Caravan Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Dodge Grand Caravan and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Dodge Grand Caravan 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.6L Pentastar ERB replaced older 3.8L units in 2011, delivering 283hp and FlexFuel capability to SXT, RT and Crew variants - but also introducing rocker arm and cylinder head failure patterns that dominate UK replacement searches.

commonFailurePoints: Head gasket degradation causing overheating; P0339/P0344 sensor circuit faults leading to stalling

fuelTypes.h2: Dodge Grand Caravan Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Dodge Grand Caravan engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from economical 3.3L to high-output 4.0L Pentastar units.

items.title: Dodge Grand Caravan Petrol Engines
items.description: All UK-market Grand Caravans use petrol V6 engines, with the 3.6L Pentastar (ERB) dominating replacement enquiries due to its prevalence in commonly imported 2011-2016 models.
items.descriptor: All UK-market Grand Caravans use petrol V6 engines, with the 3.6L Pentastar (ERB) dominating replacement enquiries due to its prevalence in commonly imported 2011-2016 models.
items.familiesLabel: Common Dodge Grand Caravan Petrol Engine Families
families: ERB (3.6L Pentastar) - Most common replacement engine (SXT/RT/Crew), rocker arm failure Grand Caravank
EGH/EGL (3.8L V6) - Pre-2011 workhorse, head gasket and stalling issues drive demand
EGV/EGA (3.3L V6) - Entry-level option, timing chain and oil pressure concerns on high mileage
EGQ (4.0L V6) - RT-only high-output unit, specialist rebuild requirements due to rarity
knownFor: Strong low-end torque suitable for family/mpv use
ERB Pentastar rocker arm failures between 60k-100k miles
EGH/EGL head gasket issues on neglected cooling systems
High UK replacement demand on SXT and RT trims due to import volumes
items.cta: Compare Dodge Grand Caravan Petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Dodge Grand Caravan Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Dodge Grand Caravan's UK-import production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The earliest UK-import Grand Caravans used simple pushrod V6 engines, offering reliability but requiring careful maintenance as mileage accumulates.
keyChanges: Introduction of FlexFuel capability on select 3.3L EGA/EGV engines
Transition from 3.3L-only to 3.8L EGH availability in higher trims
AWD option available with 3.8L EGH through 2004
knownFor: Simple pushrod design easier to service than later overhead-cam units
Head gasket failures common on 3.8L EGH above 90k miles
Timing chain tensioner wear on 3.3L units requires proactive replacement
Good parts availability from US donors supports UK rebuilds
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2006 Dodge Grand Caravan replacement engines
years.ticker: Grand Caravan 3.8 head gasket replacement • 3.3L timing chain rattle fix • SE engine stalling at idle

years.description: The RT platform brought updated styling and the high-output 4.0L EGQ to RT trims, while 3.8L EGH/EGL remained the volume seller for SXT and SE variants.
keyChanges: Introduction of RT trim with exclusive 4.0L EGQ engine option
EGL calibration update for 3.8L engines in 2007-2010 SXT models
Enhanced electronics increasing P0339/P0344 sensor fault prevalence
knownFor: 3.8L head gasket failures peak in this generation due to age and mileage
RT 4.0L EGQ offers strong performance but limited UK donor availability
Sensor-related stalling (P0339/P0344) becomes common complaint in owner forums
Good candidate for rebuilt engine upgrades addressing known weaknesses
years.cta: Compare prices for 2007-2010 Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement
years.ticker: SXT 3.8 engine replacement cost • RT 4.0 specialist rebuild • Grand Caravan P0344 code fix

years.description: The introduction of the 3.6L Pentastar ERB in 2011 transformed Grand Caravan performance and efficiency, but also introduced new failure patterns that now drive the majority of UK replacement searches.
keyChanges: Replacement of 3.8L with 3.6L Pentastar ERB across all trims
Standard FlexFuel capability and 283hp output on all variants
Updated engine management reducing sensor faults but introducing rocker arm concerns
mainEngines: ERB - 3.6L Pentastar petrol (SXT, RT, Crew, SE 2011-2016)
knownFor: ERB rocker arm failure causes distinctive ticking noise from 60k miles
Cylinder head cracks on 2011-2014 units require specialist assessment
Most commonly imported generation to the UK, driving highest replacement demand
Rebuilt ERB engines with upgraded rocker arms offer best long-term solution
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2016 Dodge Grand Caravan Pentastar engine replacement
years.ticker: Pentastar ERB ticking noise fix • SXT 3.6 cylinder head replacement • Crew van engine supply and fit
enquiries: Crew van engine supply and fit

faq.h2: Dodge Grand Caravan Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is my Dodge Grand Caravan engine worth replacing?
items.answer: For most UK-import Grand Caravans valued over £2,500, engine replacement is cost-effective versus vehicle write-off. Rebuilt engines from our network typically cost £1,450-£4,200 supply-only, with supply & fit adding £650-£1,400 labour.
keyPoints: Rebuilt ERB Pentastar engines start from £1,850 supply-only
Supply & fit service available nationwide through vetted specialists
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Compare quotes before committing to ensure best value
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Dodge Grand Caravan engine prices for your variant

items.question: What engines fit a Dodge Grand Caravan?
items.answer: Only petrol V6 engines were fitted to UK-market Grand Caravans: 3.3L (EGA/EGV), 3.6L Pentastar (ERB), 3.8L (EGH/EGL), and 4.0L (EGQ). Compatibility depends on model year and variant - verify via VIN before ordering.
keyPoints: 2000-2006: 3.3L EGA/EGV or 3.8L EGH
2007-2010: 3.8L EGL/EGH or 4.0L EGQ (RT only)
2011-2016: 3.6L ERB Pentastar exclusively
Always confirm engine code matches your vehicle's VIN plate
items.cta: Get quotes for your exact Dodge Grand Caravan engine code

items.question: How much does a Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical UK rebuilt engine prices range from £1,100 (3.3L) to £4,200 (3.6L Pentastar) supply-only. Supply & fit adds £650-£1,400 labour depending on location and workshop rates.
keyPoints: 3.3L rebuilt: £1,100-£2,800 supply-only
3.6L ERB rebuilt: £1,850-£4,200 supply-only
3.8L EGH/EGL rebuilt: £1,450-£3,600 supply-only
Labour for supply & fit typically £650-£1,400 nationwide
items.cta: Compare live Dodge Grand Caravan engine prices from UK suppliers

items.question: What causes the Dodge Grand Caravan 3.6L Pentastar ticking noise?
items.answer: The distinctive ticking on ERB Pentastar engines is usually rocker arm wear, which can progress to cylinder head damage if ignored. Early intervention with a rocker arm service may prevent full engine replacement.
keyPoints: Rocker arm failure typically occurs between 60k-100k miles
Untreated ticking can lead to cylinder head cracks and misfires
Rebuilt ERB engines include upgraded rocker arms addressing this weakness
Diagnostic inspection recommended before committing to replacement
items.cta: Find rebuilt ERB Pentastar engines with upgraded rocker arms

items.question: Can I replace a 3.8L Grand Caravan engine with a 3.6L Pentastar?
items.answer: Engine swaps between 3.8L and 3.6L are not recommended for UK Grand Caravans due to ECU, wiring harness and mounting differences. Always replace with the original engine code specified for your variant and year.
keyPoints: ECU calibration and sensor layouts differ between engine families
Mounting points and ancillary fittings are not directly compatible
UK insurance and MOT compliance requires like-for-like replacement
Our network supplies exact-match rebuilt engines for your VIN
items.cta: Get a like-for-like rebuilt engine quote for your Grand Caravan

items.question: How long does a Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical turnaround is 2-4 days from order to completion: 1-2 days for engine dispatch from supplier, plus 1-2 days for professional fitting at a UK specialist workshop.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines usually dispatched within 24-48 hours of order confirmation
Professional fitting takes 6-10 hours depending on variant and accessibility
Post-installation diagnostics and road test included in supply & fit service
Nationwide delivery means most UK locations covered within 48 hours
items.cta: Book supply & fit for your Dodge Grand Caravan engine replacement

items.question: Do Dodge Grand Caravan replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes - every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Used engines typically carry 1-3 month coverage from breakers.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Used engines from breakers typically include 1-3 month coverage
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Dodge Grand Caravan engine quotes

items.question: What is the most reliable engine for a Dodge Grand Caravan?
items.answer: The 3.6L Pentastar ERB (2011-2016) offers the best balance of power, efficiency and parts availability when rebuilt with upgraded rocker arms. Earlier 3.3L units are simpler but less powerful and more prone to timing chain wear.
keyPoints: ERB Pentastar: 283hp, widely available, but requires rocker arm upgrade
3.3L EGA/EGV: Simple design, lower power, timing chain maintenance critical
3.8L EGH/EGL: Reliable if cooling system maintained, head gasket prone on high mileage
Rebuilt engines with addressed failure points offer best long-term reliability
items.cta: Find the most reliable rebuilt engine option for your Grand Caravan

items.question: How do I find my Dodge Grand Caravan engine code?
items.answer: Your engine code is stamped on the engine block near the oil pan, and also listed on the vehicle's VIN plate under the bonnet or on the driver's door shut. Enter your registration above for instant code verification via DVLA.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near oil pan (ERB, EGH, EGL, EGV, EGA, EGQ)
VIN plate under bonnet or driver's door shut also lists engine specification
DVLA lookup via registration provides instant code confirmation
Our compatibility checker matches your code to available replacement options
items.cta: Identify your engine code instantly with our registration lookup

items.question: Should I choose a used or rebuilt Dodge Grand Caravan engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines offer better long-term value for Grand Caravans intended for continued use, with upgraded components addressing known failures and a 12-month warranty. Used engines suit tight budgets or short-term needs.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: £1,100-£4,200, upgraded internals, 12-month warranty
Used: £950-£2,400, mileage-dependent, 1-3 month warranty typical
Rebuilt recommended for vans over 80k miles or with service history gaps
Used suitable for low-mileage donors or temporary solutions
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Dodge Grand Caravan engine options and prices
